name: Capital Coin & Bullion
description_short: Austin-based precious metals and collectible coin dealer buying, selling, and trading gold, silver, platinum, and palladium since 2009. Direct sourcing and low overhead enable competitive pricing.
description_long:
Capital Coin & Bullion was established in 2009 by Chad and Becky Tyson in Austin, Texas, after they transitioned from an antique business in Fredericksburg. The founders' longstanding interest in coin history and currency evolved into a full-service precious metals operation catering to both casual buyers and experienced collectors. Chad's coin collecting began in childhood alongside his father, and the business reflects his vision to provide Austin with a reputable community hub for numismatists and bullion investors.

The company buys, sells, and trades physical precious metals including gold, silver, platinum, and palladium, along with collectible coins. They display real-time spot prices on their website and maintain a physical retail location with standard retail hours (Monday-Friday 10am-6pm, Saturday 10am-2pm) and separate buying hours (Monday-Friday 10am-5pm, closed Saturdays for purchases). Their inventory model relies on direct purchases from the public rather than wholesale suppliers, allowing them to stock both bullion and numismatic items.

Capital Coin & Bullion differentiates itself through operational efficiency: low overhead as a small business, direct public sourcing of inventory, word-of-mouth reputation building, and emphasis on customer education and community comfort. The team includes ownership (Chad & Becky Tyson), manager Melanie Kline (employed since 2019), and Jeremy Marty (joined early 2024 from automotive sales). The company explicitly warns customers that it does not solicit unsolicited calls to buy, positioning itself against predatory telemarketing practices common in the precious metals industry.

The business model is straightforward and sustainable for collectors and investors seeking to buy or liquidate precious metals locally without intermediaries. However, as a small retail operation without online shipping capability mentioned on their website, customers must conduct transactions in person in Austin. The company's Google reviews are cited as a strength, though the website content provided does not include specific rating data or detailed customer testimonials beyond one partial review excerpt.
pros: Real-time spot prices for gold, silver, platinum, and palladium displayed on homepage
Direct buying from public reduces markup—inventory sourced directly rather than through wholesale suppliers
Low overhead operational model enables competitive pricing claims backed by business model transparency
Staff education focus: reviews explicitly mention customers learning about coins and currency with each transaction
Explicit anti-scam warning shows consumer protection awareness and transparency about predatory industry practices
Established 15+ year track record (since 2009) with founded team still in operation
Separate buying hours clarified (Mon-Fri 10am-5pm only) prevents customer confusion about when to sell
cons: Local in-person transaction only—no online sales or shipping capability mentioned; limits customer reach
Limited buying availability—not open Saturdays for purchases, restricting seller flexibility
No transparency on markup or bid-ask spread between their buy and sell prices on the website
Placeholder Lorem ipsum text visible on website ('How we beat the big boys' section), suggesting incomplete or outdated web maintenance
No published information about authentication processes, guarantees, or return policies for transactions
best_for: Austin-area precious metals collectors seeking to buy or sell bullion locally without shipping
Numismatists interested in learning coin history and currency from experienced, education-focused staff
Individuals liquidating inherited coins or bullion who prefer face-to-face transactions with established local business
Bullion stackers and investors seeking competitive spot pricing from a low-markup community operation
diagnosis: Capital Coin & Bullion is best for Austin-area collectors, investors, and individuals seeking to buy or sell precious metals in person with a small, established local business offering competitive pricing through low overhead and direct sourcing. The main caveat is that transactions are in-person only with limited buying hours (weekdays only, no Saturday purchases), making it inaccessible for remote customers or those needing weekend transaction flexibility.
services: Gold bullion buying and selling at live spot prices
Silver bullion buying and selling at live spot prices
Platinum bullion buying and selling at live spot prices
Palladium bullion buying and selling at live spot prices
Coin and bullion trading (exchange of precious metals)
Direct public acquisition of inventory (customers can sell directly to business)
Customer education on coin history and precious metals
